name: Sotonera Reservoir Loop
name_original: Embalse de la Sotonera — vuelta gravel desde Alcalá de Gurrea
description: Enjoy an easy 13.8-mile gravel cycling tour around Embalse de la Sotonera from Alcalá de Gurrea, featuring reservoir views and birdwatching.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Alcalá De Gurrea are characterized by a blend of open plains, the prominent La Sotonera Reservoir, and gentle hills within the Hoya de Huesca region. The landscape offers expansive views across the plains of La Violada and La Sotonera, providing long stretches suitable for touring cyclists. Unique geological formations like the Aguarales de Valdemilaz add natural interest, while distant views of the Sierra de Guara provide a mountainous backdrop to the otherwise flatter routes. Best touring cycling routes around Alcalá De Gurrea The most popular touring cycling route is Embalse de la Sotonera — gravel tour from Alcalá de Gurrea, an easy 13.8 miles (22.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 50 minutes to complete, circling the scenic La Sotonera Reservoir. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Sotonera Reservoir – La Sotonera Reservoir loop from Club Náutico La Sotonera, a moderate 21.3 miles (34.3 km) path offering continuous views of the reservoir and its surroundings. Local touring cyclists also love the Sotonera Reservoir – Ardisa Dam loop from Puendeluna, a 29.7 miles (47.7 km) trail leading through varied terrain between two significant water features, often completed in about 3 hours 33 minutes. Touring cycling around Alcalá De Gurrea is defined by the vast plains, the expansive La Sotonera Reservoir, and unique geological formations. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy routes around the reservoir to more challenging loops. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 100 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Alcalá De Gurrea's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Alcalá De Gurrea?
A: There are over 15 touring cycling routes around Alcalá De Gurrea, offering a variety of distances and difficulty levels to explore the Hoya de Huesca region.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of the touring cycling routes in this area?
A: The routes vary in difficulty, catering to different experience levels. You'll find 1 easy route, 9 moderate routes, and 6 more challenging routes, ensuring there's something for everyone.

Q: What natural attractions can I explore while cycling near Alcalá De Gurrea?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can cycle around the prominent &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3562656" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Sotonera Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, visit the unique geological formations of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/558941" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguarales de Valdemilaz&lt;/a&gt;, or pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5857443" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ardisa Dam&lt;/a&gt;. The extensive plains of La Violada and La Sotonera also offer expansive views and opportunities for birdwatching.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural sites accessible by bike in the region?
A: Yes, Alcalá de Gurrea itself has historical points of interest like the Parish Church of San Jorge. You can also visit &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5284575" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Atalaya (watchtower in La Sotonera)&lt;/a&gt;, an Islamic watchtower overlooking the reservoir. The nearby Colonia de Tormos, with its early 20th-century modernist architecture, is another interesting stop.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes around Alcalá De Gurrea?
A: Absolutely. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979471478/embalse-de-la-sotonera-gravel-tour-from-alcala-de-gurrea" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de la Sotonera — gravel tour from Alcalá de Gurrea&lt;/a&gt; are classified as easy, making them suitable for families looking for a pleasant ride around the scenic reservoir.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in Alcalá De Gurrea?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14941724" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sotonera Reservoir – La Sotonera Reservoir loop from Club Náutico La Sotonera&lt;/a&gt; offers continuous views, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38183653" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sotonera Reservoir – Ardisa Dam loop from Puendeluna&lt;/a&gt; takes you through varied terrain between two significant water features.

Q: When is the best time of year for touring cycling in Alcalá De Gurrea?
A: The open plains and reservoir areas are generally pleasant for cycling during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be hot, but early mornings or late afternoons are still enjoyable. Winter offers a different, often quieter, experience, though you should check local weather conditions.

Q: What kind of views can I expect on the touring cycling routes?
A: From elevated points, you'll enjoy expansive views across the plains of La Violada and La Sotonera. Many routes offer continuous vistas of the La Sotonera Reservoir. On clear days, you can even catch distant views of the dramatic Sierra de Guara and Mallos de Riglos, providing a stunning mountainous backdrop.

Q: Are there options for longer, more challenging touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, for those seeking a greater challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17546401" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almudévar – Alcalá de Gurrea loop from Almudévar&lt;/a&gt;, at over 60 km, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38184795" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino de Loreto – Sotonera Reservoir loop from Alcalá de Gurrea&lt;/a&gt;, which is over 67 km, offer significant distances and elevation gains, suitable for experienced touring cyclists.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Alcalá De Gurrea?
A: The komoot community frequently praises the varied terrain, from the accessible plains to the scenic La Sotonera Reservoir. Cyclists appreciate the blend of natural wonders with cultural and historical sites, and the ability to find routes suitable for different ability levels.

Q: Is it possible to reach Alcalá De Gurrea with public transport if I bring my bike?
A: While specific public transport options for bikes can vary, the Huesca province generally has bus services connecting smaller towns. It's advisable to check with local bus operators for their current policies regarding bicycle transport to Alcalá De Gurrea.

Q: Where can I find parking for my car when starting a cycling tour?
A: Alcalá De Gurrea, as a small municipality, typically offers street parking. For routes starting from specific points like Club Náutico La Sotonera or other villages, designated parking areas are often available. It's recommended to check the starting point of your chosen route for specific parking information.
tips:
The ruins of the Watchtower tower, probably built between the 9th and 10th centuries, are of Islamic origin. The tower, with a square plan, preserves only three walls, since the south wall collapsed. It is also known as the Four Corners Tower.

It is located in a privileged place, on a hill that overlooks the Sotonera reservoir. Its elevated position allowed visual communication with the Tower of San Mitiel or the Bolea castle.

The Sotonera reservoir belongs to the Ebro basin in the municipality of Alcalá de Gurrea. In the same way as the municipality of La Sotonera, it takes its name from the Sotón river, which is the most important, together with the Astón river, the Alboré basin and a derivation of the Gàllego river that contributes approximately 86% of its volume Its reservoir is the source of the Canal dels Monegros. Because of its fauna it has become an area of interest, and the birds in particular are listed as being of international importance.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Embalse de la Sotonera gravel tour from Alcalá de Gurrea?
faqs.answer: This route is generally considered easy, making it ideal for those new to gravel cycling or looking for a relaxed touring bicycle ride. Komoot categorizes it as an 'easy bike ride'.

faqs.question: Is the Embalse de la Sotonera — gravel tour from Alcalá de Gurrea a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, this is a classic and beautiful circular route around the Embalse de la Sotonera, starting and ending near Alcalá de Gurrea.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this gravel tour?
faqs.answer: The route features a varied terrain, primarily consisting of gravel tracks (pistas) and local paved roads. Be aware that sections directly alongside the reservoir can sometimes be muddy or waterlogged due to water level fluctuations. There are also short excavated paths between earthen walls.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll enjoy continuous views of the Embalse de la Sotonera. Key highlights include the impressive The Atalaya (watchtower in La Sotonera) and the Sotonera Reservoir itself, which is a significant wetland. The Alberca de Alboré is also an excellent spot for birdwatching.

faqs.question: Is the trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail is generally accessible, there are no specific regulations prohibiting dogs. However, as it passes through natural wetland areas and agricultural lands, it's advisable to keep dogs on a leash to protect local wildlife, especially migratory birds, and respect private property.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Embalse de la Sotonera gravel tour. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best times to enjoy this gravel tour are during spring and autumn. These seasons offer pleasant temperatures and the unique opportunity to observe thousands of cranes during their migratory stopovers in late winter and early autumn.

faqs.question: Are there any places to get refreshments or water along the way?
faqs.answer: You can find bars in the towns of Alcalá de Gurrea and Montmesa, as well as at the Club Náutico La Sotonera and Camping de la Sotonera. However, there are no water points directly along the route, so it's highly recommended to carry sufficient water with you.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Embalse de la Sotonera gravel tour?
faqs.answer: The tour starts from Alcalá de Gurrea, where you can find suitable parking to begin your ride. Parking is generally available within the town, providing convenient access to the route.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several local paths. You will cycle along parts of the Camino de Almudévar a Montmesa, Camino del Albergue, and Camino de Almudévar a Tormos.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The Embalse de la Sotonera is a significant wetland, particularly known as a stopover for thousands of cranes during their migratory routes in late winter and early autumn. It's an excellent area for birdwatching in general.
